key_topics: Multiplication: facts 0-12, multiplication tables mastery
Division: basic facts, relationship to multiplication
Multi-digit multiplication: two-digit by one-digit
Long division: one-digit divisors
Fractions: numerator and denominator, fractions on a number line
Equivalent fractions: finding and comparing
Adding and subtracting fractions with like denominators
Place value: to 10,000
Rounding: nearest 10, 100, 1000
Measurement: area using unit squares, perimeter
Elapsed time: calculating time intervals
Geometry: quadrilaterals, right angles
Graphs: line plots, pictographs, bar graphs
competences: Know multiplication and division facts 0-12 from memory
Find equivalent fractions and compare fractions
Calculate area and perimeter of rectangles
Read and create line plots with fractional units
Solve word problems using all four operations

key_topics: Reading: chapter books, myths, folktales, biographies
Comprehension: theme, character motivation, cause and effect
Figurative language: simile, metaphor, personification
Point of view: first vs. third person
Writing: opinion writing with supporting reasons
Narrative writing: well-structured stories with dialogue
Informational writing: paragraphs with topic sentences
Research: gathering information from texts and reference materials
Grammar: common and proper nouns, adjectives, adverbs, prepositions
Sentences: simple, compound, and complex
Spelling: Greek and Latin roots, prefixes, suffixes
competences: Identify theme and character motivation in grade-level literature
Write opinion essays with organised reasons and conclusions
Write narrative stories with dialogue and descriptive detail
Use compound and complex sentences in writing
typical_mistakes: Theme confusion: stating topic instead of theme (e.g., 'dogs' instead of 'loyalty is important')
Not using transition words between paragraphs

key_topics: Ancient Greece: city-states, Athens and Sparta, democracy
Greek mythology: gods and heroes (Zeus, Athena, Odyssey)
Ancient Rome: Republic, Empire, Julius Caesar, Augustus
Roman contributions: law, architecture, language
World Geography: Europe — major countries and capitals
American History: the Civil War — causes and consequences
Abraham Lincoln and the Emancipation Proclamation
Reconstruction: changes and challenges after the Civil War
competences: Compare Athens and Sparta as political systems
Describe major contributions of ancient Greece and Rome
Explain causes and consequences of the Civil War
Locate European countries on a map
typical_mistakes: Confusing Greek and Roman gods (Zeus/Jupiter, Poseidon/Neptune)

key_topics: Life Science: ecosystems, food webs, energy flow
Adaptations: physical and behavioural adaptations
Habitats: biomes of North America
Earth Science: weather instruments — thermometer, barometer, anemometer
Water cycle: evaporation, condensation, precipitation
Rocks and minerals: classification, rock cycle
Physical Science: simple machines — lever, pulley, inclined plane, wheel and axle
Forces: gravity, friction, mass vs. weight
competences: Explain energy flow through a food web
Describe the water cycle with correct vocabulary
Classify rocks as igneous, sedimentary, or metamorphic
Identify and explain simple machines in everyday life
typical_mistakes: Thinking the water cycle has a starting point
